message:
feat(core): add r_tree.rs — spatial index for 2D rectangle queries (ft-283h4.40)

R-tree with minimum-enlargement insertion and sorted-split node overflow.
Supports range query, point query, nearest neighbor search with branch
pruning. Arena-allocated nodes with configurable MAX_ENTRIES/MIN_ENTRIES.
17 inline tests + 12 property-based tests against brute-force reference.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ft-1bznu.4","title":"[Subtask wa-1bznu] Register task_spawn bench target and add missing structured-concurrency invariants tests","description":"Add missing Cargo bench target registration for crates/frankenterm-core/benches/task_spawn.rs and expand asupersync infrastructure tests to explicitly cover structured-concurrency invariants required by wa-1bznu (scope/child completion and cancellation semantics via available helper APIs). Keep scope non-overlapping with wa-1bznu.2.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GoldForge","created_at":"2026-02-15T01:10:41.874166Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-15T01:17:34.575419Z","closed_at":"2026-02-15T01:17:34.575405Z","close_reason":"Completed: task_spawn bench target registered and structured-concurrency invariant tests added with targeted validation evidence","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-1bznu.4","depends_on_id":"ft-1bznu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-15T21:29:20Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":38,"issue_id":"ft-1bznu.4","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Completed wa-1bznu.4 non-overlap slice.

Code changes:
- crates/frankenterm-core/Cargo.toml
  - Added explicit bench target registration:
    [[bench]]
    name = "task_spawn"
    harness = false
    required-features = ["asupersync-runtime"]
- crates/frankenterm-core/tests/asupersync_cx_infrastructure.rs
  - Added structured-concurrency invariant tests:
    1) spawn_bounded_helper_clamps_zero_concurrency_to_one
    2) spawn_bounded_children_observe_parent_cancellation

Targeted validation (with rch diagnose evidence showing WOULD INTERCEPT):
- CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target_goldforge_wa1bznu4 cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test asupersync_cx_infrastructure --features asupersync-runtime -- --nocapture
  - PASS (8 passed)
- CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target_goldforge_wa1bznu4 cargo bench -p frankenterm-core --bench task_spawn --features asupersync-runtime --no-run
  - PASS (bench target builds)

Workspace gates run per policy (failures are pre-existing/unrelated to this slice):
- cargo check --workspace --all-targets
  - FAIL at existing tests in crates/frankenterm-core/tests/proptest_kalman_watchdog.rs and crates/frankenterm-core/tests/proptest_environment.rs
- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings
  - FAIL on existing warnings/lints in unrelated crates/files (e.g., rangeset, portable-pty, frankenterm-uds, frankenterm-blob-leases, frankenterm-core/src/search/embedder.rs)
- cargo fmt --check
  - FAIL on existing formatting drift in unrelated files (e.g., crates/frankenterm-core/src/diagnostic.rs, output/error_renderer.rs, explanations.rs)
","created_at":"2026-02-15T01:17:31Z"}]}

Keeping bead in_progress pending successful offloaded validation evidence.","created_at":"2026-02-15T01:20:31Z"},{"id":41,"issue_id":"ft-1bznu.5","author":"PearlBasin","text":"Completion update (PearlBasin): Code change landed in crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp.rs replacing direct tokio runtime builder with runtime_compat::RuntimeBuilder in record_mcp_audit_sync(). Keep scope to kalman_watchdog/environment test+type surfaces only.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GoldForge","created_at":"2026-02-15T01:18:44.531237Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-15T01:24:29.487220Z","closed_at":"2026-02-15T01:24:29.487189Z","close_reason":"Completed: fixed kalman Option-return assertion and environment Deserialize roundtrip support; workspace cargo check now passes","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-1bznu.6","depends_on_id":"ft-1bznu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-15T21:29:20Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":42,"issue_id":"ft-1bznu.6","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Completed wa-1bznu.6 compile-unblock slice.

Code changes:
- crates/frankenterm-core/tests/proptest_kalman_watchdog.rs
  - Updated classify_component property assertion to handle current API (`Option<HealthClassification>`), asserting `Some` for tracked components before status checks.
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/environment.rs
  - Added `Deserialize` derives to roundtrip-relevant types:
    - ConnectionType
    - ConfigSource
    - ConfigRecommendation
    - AutoConfig
  - Updated serde import to `use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};`

Validation (with rch diagnose showing WOULD INTERCEPT for cargo check/clippy):
- CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target_goldforge_wa1bznu6 cargo check -p frankenterm-core --test proptest_kalman_watchdog ✅
- CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target_goldforge_wa1bznu6 cargo check -p frankenterm-core --test proptest_environment ✅
- CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target_goldforge_wa1bznu6 cargo check --workspace --all-targets ✅ (warnings only)
- CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target_goldforge_wa1bznu6 cargo clippy -p frankenterm-core --test proptest_kalman_watchdog --test proptest_environment -- -D warnings ✅
- cargo fmt --check ❌ fails on pre-existing unrelated formatting drift in other files (e.g., dataflow.rs, diagnostic.rs, explanations.rs, output/error_renderer.rs, test_artifacts.rs)
","created_at":"2026-02-15T01:24:25Z"}]}
@@ -198,7 +198,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-1u90p.4.10","title":"[Subtask wa-1u90p.4.2] Implement rollback/fallback handling for failed frame commits","description":"## Scope
Add robust rollback path when a candidate frame fails validation/commit.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Failure-injection tests verify clean fallback to last-good frame.
- No user-visible artifact leaks during rollback scenarios.
- Telemetry captures rollback cause taxonomy.","notes":"Implemented rollback/fallback handling for failed resize frame commits in frankenterm/term/src/screen.rs. Added rollback cause taxonomy via LastGoodFrameRollbackCause (resize_commit_validation, forced_failure_injection) and structured telemetry logs: last_good_frame_rollback cause=<...> action=<applied|missing_snapshot>. Added rollback lifecycle counters (rollback_count, rollback_missing_snapshot_count) and snapshot carry-over of dpi for complete restoration. Wired resize commit failure path to rollback to retained last-good frame and added deterministic failure injection for tests. New tests: last_good_frame_rolls_back_after_forced_resize_commit_failure, last_good_frame_rollback_tracks_missing_snapshot_failures. Validation: rustfmt --edition 2024 frankenterm/term/src/screen.rs; CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-swiftdeer cargo test -p frankenterm-term last_good_frame_ -- --nocapture; CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-swiftdeer cargo check -p frankenterm-term --all-targets; CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-swiftdeer cargo clippy -p frankenterm-term --lib -- -D warnings.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SwiftDeer","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2026-02-13T01:16:04.212574Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-14T16:52:41.365901Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T16:52:41.364616Z","close_reason":"Added rollback-to-last-good-frame fallback with cause taxonomy telemetry and failure-injection coverage for failed resize commit scenarios","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fallback","reflow","render","resize","subtask","track-render"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-1u90p.4.10","depends_on_id":"ft-1u90p.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-15T21:29:20Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1u90p.4.10","depends_on_id":"ft-1u90p.4.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-15T21:29:20Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-1u90p.4.2","title":"Implement last-good-frame hold and atomic frame swap for resize transitions","description":"Guarantee users never see unstable intermediate layout by presenting only coherent frames. Preserve visual continuity while background reflow converges.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Implementation is complete for the stated scope and behavior is validated.
- Unit tests are added/updated for core logic and edge cases.
- Integration and/or e2e coverage is updated where this task affects multi-component behavior.
- Structured telemetry/logging is added for observability and regression triage.
- No regressions in existing terminal semantics within the affected scope.","notes":"Integrated the render/presentation artifact-suppression stack across completed subtasks wa-1u90p.4.8/4.9/4.10: (1) last-good-frame retention/invalidation lifecycle bookkeeping with bounded memory and lineage logs (frankenterm/term/src/screen.rs), (2) atomic present-commit protocol + swap barrier checks + commit/reject telemetry in pane resize worker (frankenterm/mux/src/localpane.rs), and (3) rollback/fallback handling to retained last-good frame for failed resize commit paths with rollback cause taxonomy telemetry (last_good_frame_rollback cause=<...> action=<...>). Added/validated tests proving coherent-frame behavior and fallback safety: localpane replay present-commit rejection/commit tests plus screen rollback failure-injection tests. Validation runs: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-swiftdeer cargo test -p frankenterm-term last_good_frame_ -- --nocapture; CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-swiftdeer cargo test -p mux replay_present -- --nocapture; CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-swiftdeer cargo check -p frankenterm-term --all-targets; CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-swiftdeer cargo check -p mux --all-targets; CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-swiftdeer cargo clippy -p frankenterm-term --lib -- -D warnings; CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-swiftdeer cargo clippy -p mux --all-targets -- -D warnings (completed earlier after localpane commit protocol changes).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SwiftDeer","estimated_minutes":360,"created_at":"2026-02-13T00:25:45.699828Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-14T16:54:05.452163Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T16:54:05.452147Z","close_reason":"Integrated and validated last-good-frame retention, atomic present-commit barriering, and rollback fallback for failed frame commits","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["gpu","presentation","reflow","render","resize","track-render"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-1u90p.4.2","depends_on_id":"ft-1u90p.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-15T21:29:20Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1u90p.4.2","depends_on_id":"ft-1u90p.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-15T21:29:20Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1u90p.4.2","depends_on_id":"ft-1u90p.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-15T21:29:20Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1u90p.4.2","depends_on_id":"ft-1u90p.4.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-15T21:29:20Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1u90p.4.2","depends_on_id":"ft-1u90p.4.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-15T21:29:20Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1u90p.4.2","depends_on_id":"ft-1u90p.4.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-15T21:29:20Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-1u90p.4.3","title":"Implement dirty-rect/tile rendering and partial GPU uploads","description":"Restrict GPU/CPU work to changed regions and avoid full-frame rebuild when unnecessary. Measure bandwidth and frame-time improvements under churn.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Implementation is complete for the stated scope and behavior is validated.
- Unit tests are added/updated for core logic and edge cases.
- Integration and/or e2e coverage is updated where this task affects multi-component behavior.
- Structured telemetry/logging is added for observability and regression triage.
- No regressions in existing terminal semantics within the affected scope.","notes":"2026-02-14 SageReef: Progress slice on wa-1u90p.4.3 landed in frankenterm/surface/src/lib.rs.

What changed:
- Added draw-path integration helpers for dirty metadata:
  - draw_from_screen_with_dirty_rects(&mut self, other, x, y) -> (SequenceNo, Vec<DirtyRect>)
  - draw_from_screen_with_dirty_tiles(&mut self, other, x, y, tile_width, tile_height) -> (SequenceNo, Vec<DirtyRect>)
- Updated draw_from_screen() to route through draw_from_screen_with_dirty_rects() for single-path behavior.
- Added/validated dirty-diff metadata primitives used by these helpers (DirtyRect APIs and region/tile extraction).
- Added new tests:
  - draw_from_screen_with_dirty_rects_reports_regions
  - draw_from_screen_with_dirty_tiles_reports_tile_aligned_regions

Validation:
- rustfmt frankenterm/surface/src/lib.rs
- CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-sagereef-wa-1u90p-4-3 cargo test -p frankenterm-surface
  - Result: 282 passed, 0 failed.

Status:
- Bead remains in_progress. Next integration needed is plumbing these dirty rect/tile outputs into the runtime render/upload telemetry path used by resize performance instrumentation.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"QuietValley","estimated_minutes":360,"created_at":"2026-02-13T00:25:45.803592Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-16T02:58:31.631230Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dirty-rect","gpu","reflow","render","resize","track-render"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-1u90p.4.3","depends_on_id":"ft-1u90p.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-15T21:29:20Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1u90p.4.3","depends_on_id":"ft-1u90p.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-15T21:29:20Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1u90p.4.3","depends_on_id":"ft-1u90p.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-15T21:29:20Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":109,"issue_id":"ft-1u90p.4.3","author":"LavenderLynx","text":"LavenderLynx support slice started (non-overlapping with frankenterm/surface work): wiring dirty range metadata into vendored mux/tailer telemetry path and tests. Reserved crates/frankenterm-core/src/vendored/mux_client.rs + crates/frankenterm-core/src/tailer.rs; will report validation and release reservations on completion.","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:25:42Z"},{"id":110,"issue_id":"ft-1u90p.4.3","author":"LavenderLynx","text":"LavenderLynx support slice completed: added dirty_row_count propagation from vendored render change ranges into PaneDelta::Output and streaming bridge telemetry string; added total_dirty_rows unit tests in mux_client and updated tailer streaming delta test assertions. Validation: vendored lib check + targeted tests passed; cargo check/clippy all-targets passed; cargo fmt --check currently fails on unrelated pre-existing formatting drift in other test files.","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:34:42Z"},{"id":111,"issue_id":"ft-1u90p.4.3","author":"LavenderLynx","text":"LavenderLynx follow-up support slice completed: (1) StreamingBridge now tracks aggregate dirty_range_total + dirty_row_total with getters and StreamingHealth fields; tests updated for default/accumulation behavior.  {"id":"ft-283h4.3","title":"Formal mux protocol verification via TLA+","description":"# Formal Mux Protocol Verification via TLA+

## Why This Is Novel
No terminal emulator in existence has formal protocol verification. This is the purest expression of alien-artifact-coding. TLA+ finds bugs that no amount of testing can find — Lamport proved this mathematically. Amazon uses TLA+ for S3, DynamoDB, EBS, and found critical bugs in all of them.

## What We Model

### Spec 1: PDU Exchange Protocol
States: Idle, Sending, Receiving, Error, Reconnecting
Properties to verify:
- **Safety**: Messages are never lost (every sent PDU is eventually received or error-reported)
- **Ordering**: PDUs are delivered in sequence order
- **No duplicates**: Each PDU is processed exactly once
- **Liveness**: Every operation eventually completes (no indefinite blocking)

### Spec 2: Snapshot/Restore Lifecycle
States: Normal, Capturing, Writing, Stored, Restoring, Restored
Properties to verify:
- **Consistency**: Snapshot captures a consistent point-in-time state
- **Atomicity**: Restore is all-or-nothing (no partial state)
- **No data loss**: snapshot(state) followed by restore() reproduces state exactly
- **Idempotency**: restore(restore(snapshot)) == restore(snapshot)

### Spec 3: Concurrent Pane Management
Multiple panes being created, destroyed, resized, focused simultaneously.
Properties to verify:
- **No orphans**: Every created pane is eventually destroyed or persisted
- **No leaks**: Resources (FDs, memory) are freed when panes close
- **No deadlocks**: No circular wait conditions under any interleaving
- **Liveness**: Every create/destroy operation completes in bounded steps

### Spec 4: WAL Correctness (if #2 is implemented)
Properties:
- **Durability**: Mutations written to WAL survive crashes
- **Replay equivalence**: Replaying WAL produces identical state to original
- **Compaction safety**: Compaction preserves all information

## Deliverables
- `docs/formal/mux_protocol.tla` — PDU exchange spec
- `docs/formal/snapshot_lifecycle.tla` — Snapshot spec
- `docs/formal/concurrent_panes.tla` — Concurrency spec
- `docs/formal/wal_correctness.tla` — WAL spec (if applicable)
- `docs/formal/VERIFICATION_RESULTS.md` — Properties verified, model checking results, any bugs found
- The TLA+ specs serve as the AUTHORITATIVE protocol documentation

## State Space
The mux protocol has ~15-20 states, 5-8 actions per state. Model checking with TLC is tractable (millions of states in minutes). We're not verifying a distributed system — just a client-server protocol with finite state.

## Tooling
- TLA+ Toolbox or `tlc` CLI for model checking
- PlusCal (algorithmic language) for easier spec writing
- Apalache (symbolic model checker) for infinite-state properties

## Testing
- Model checking all specs with TLC — must pass with zero violations
- Any bugs found are documented and fixed in the Rust implementation
- Specs updated when protocol changes

## Cost/Benefit
- Cost: ~2-3 days for a developer familiar with TLA+
- Benefit: Mathematical certainty about protocol correctness, forever
- ROI: One prevented data-loss bug pays for the entire effort

## Depends on
- wa-2umk2 (FrankenTerm in-tree) — need to understand the protocol to model it
- Does NOT depend on implementation work — verification is orthogonal

---

## Cross-References
- **wa-2dd4s.5** (FrankenMux wire protocol): The TLA+ specs in this bead formally model the wire protocol defined in wa-2dd4s.5. Any protocol changes in wa-2dd4s.5 must be reflected here, and any bugs found by TLC model checking should feed back as protocol fixes.
- **wa-283h4.13** (Self-stabilizing mux protocol): The self-stabilization convergence invariants should be modeled as TLA+ temporal properties here. Spec 1 (PDU exchange) should include self-stabilization reconciliation messages as additional actions.

## TLC Model Checker Configuration
Run TLC with explicit state space bounds to keep model checking tractable:
- Max states: 10M (sufficient for protocol with ~20 states × 8 actions)
- Max depth: 50 transitions (bounds liveness checking)
- Symmetry reduction: apply symmetry sets for pane IDs to reduce state space by N!
- Liveness checking: use `-lncheck` flag for temporal property verification
- CI integration: `tlc -workers auto -deadlock mux_protocol.tla` in GitHub Actions

## Runtime Property Validation via Proptest
While TLA+/TLC verifies the model, proptest validates that the Rust implementation matches the model:

```
proptest! {
    #[test]
    fn pdu_ordering_preserved(pdus in prop::collection::vec(arb_pdu(), 1..100)) {
        // Send PDUs through codec pipeline, verify arrival order matches send order
    }

    #[test]
    fn snapshot_restore_roundtrip(state in arb_mux_state()) {
        // snapshot(state) -> restore() -> assert_eq!(original, restored)
    }

    #[test]
    fn no_duplicate_delivery(pdus in prop::collection::vec(arb_pdu(), 1..50),
                             drop_indices in prop::collection::vec(0..50usize, 0..10)) {
        // Simulate drops and retransmits, verify each PDU delivered exactly once
    }

    #[test]
    fn convergence_from_arbitrary_state(
        initial in arb_corrupted_mux_state(),
        reconciliation_rounds in 1..5u32
    ) {
        // Verify protocol converges to legitimate state within bounded rounds
    }
}
```","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ScarletBarn","created_at":"2026-02-10T17:06:41.536788Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-13T09:40:33.381750Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-283h4.3","depends_on_id":"ft-283h4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-15T21:29:20Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-283h4.3","depends_on_id":"ft-2umk2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-15T21:29:20Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":190,"issue_id":"ft-283h4.3","author":"ScarletBarn","text":"Implemented formal verification artifacts under docs/formal: mux_protocol.tla (updated with bounded MaxMsgId + fairness Spec), snapshot_lifecycle.tla, concurrent_panes.tla, wal_correctness.tla, with companion TLC configs and docs/formal/VERIFICATION_RESULTS.md. TLC results: mux_protocol PASS (1,395/1,125 states), snapshot_lifecycle PASS (102/64), concurrent_panes safety PASS (22,980,985/1,748,872), wal_correctness PASS (92,841/36,408). Caveat documented: concurrent_panes liveness checks were deferred due existential fairness starvation artifacts with set-queue scheduling; liveness formulas remain in module for follow-on queue-model refinement.","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:40:33Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-283h4.30","title":"Implement rope.rs — balanced text rope for efficient scrollback editing","description":"# Rope for Large Text Manipulation

## Alien Artifact Track (ft-283h4)

Implement a Rope data structure for efficient manipulation of large text sequences using an arena-based balanced binary tree of string chunks.

## Data Structure
- Balanced binary tree where leaves hold string chunks
- O(log n) insert, delete, split, concat operations
- O(log n) index-to-character lookup
- Automatic rebalancing to maintain O(log n) depth

## Use Cases in FrankenTerm
- Scrollback buffer management for large terminal outputs
- Efficient text editing for terminal replay
- Fast substring extraction for search results
- Memory-efficient text storage with structural sharing

## Files
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/rope.rs
- crates/frankenterm-core/tests/proptest_rope.rs","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-16T02:30:28.854876Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-16T02:55:34.153275Z","closed_at":"2026-02-16T02:55:34.153265Z","close_reason":"Already implemented and committed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-283h4.30","depends_on_id":"ft-283h4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-16T02:30:28.854876Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-283h4.31","title":"Implement disjoint_intervals.rs — sorted non-overlapping interval set with merge-on-insert","description":"# Disjoint Intervals

## Alien Artifact Track (ft-283h4)

Implement a sorted set of non-overlapping intervals that automatically merges overlapping insertions.

## Use Cases
- Track active time windows for pane sessions
- Gap detection in telemetry streams
- Coalesce overlapping output capture ranges
- Resource occupancy tracking

## API Surface
- DisjointIntervals::new()
- insert(start, end) — add interval, merge overlaps
- contains(point) — point-in-range query
- intersects(start, end) — range overlap test
- gaps() — iterator over uncovered ranges
- span() — total covered length
- intervals() — iterate all intervals
- remove(start, end) — punch hole
- stats() / serde roundtrip

## Testing
- 25+ inline unit tests
- 30+ proptest properties","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-16T02:32:42.663162Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-16T02:35:50.516795Z","closed_at":"2026-02-16T02:35:50.516744Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-283h4.31","depends_on_id":"ft-283h4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-16T02:32:42.663162Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

## Alien Artifact Track (ft-283h4)

Implement a Fenwick Tree for efficient prefix sum queries and point updates in O(log n) time with O(n) space.

## Use Cases in FrankenTerm
- Cumulative metrics: running sum of bytes/events per pane
- Rank queries: how many panes have output above threshold
- Dynamic frequency tables for pattern detection
- Range sum queries for activity windowing

 {"id":"ft-283h4.38","title":"Implement lfu_cache.rs — O(1) LFU eviction cache for resource management","description":"# LFU Cache (Least Frequently Used)

## Alien Artifact Track (ft-283h4)

Implement an O(1) LFU cache using the O(1) LFU algorithm from Shah et al. (2010).

## Use Cases
- Evict least-used pane data under memory pressure
- Cache hot patterns/commands based on access frequency
- Resource pool management for infrequently used connections
- Complement to existing LRU cache for frequency-based policies

## API Surface
- LfuCache::new(capacity) — create with max capacity
- get(key) — lookup, increments frequency
- insert(key, value) — insert, evicts LFU if full
- remove(key) — explicit remove
- contains(key) — check without incrementing frequency
- len() / capacity / is_full
- stats() / config / serde roundtrip

## Testing
- 25+ inline unit tests
- 30+ proptest properties","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"CrimsonReef","created_at":"2026-02-16T02:50:09.363025Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-16T02:56:40.347868Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-283h4.38","depends_on_id":"ft-283h4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-16T02:50:09.363025Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"ft-283h4.4","title":"io_uring zero-copy mux I/O pipeline","description":"# io_uring Zero-Copy Mux I/O Pipeline

## Skills: /alien-artifact-coding (formal correctness), /extreme-software-optimization (profile-driven)

## Why This Is Novel
No existing bead covers the kernel I/O syscall layer. wa-3kxe.4 covers userspace data flow. wa-1lc2 covers protocol-level batching. wa-brc7d covers the async runtime. This covers the SYSTEM CALL layer — the boundary between userspace and kernel.

## The Problem
50 panes doing ~1100 syscalls/sec, each costing 1-5μs context switch = ~1-5ms/sec just in syscall transitions.

## io_uring Solution (Linux 5.6+)
1. **Submission batching**: 100 I/O ops in a single syscall
2. **Completion batching**: Reap 100 completions in single syscall
3. **Registered buffers**: Pre-register, kernel uses directly (zero-copy)
4. **Kernel polling**: SQPOLL mode eliminates submission syscalls
5. **Fixed files**: Pre-register FDs, eliminate fd-lookup overhead

## Platform Support — CRITICAL
- **Linux 5.6+**: Full io_uring support (primary target)
- **Linux <5.6**: Fall back to epoll
- **macOS**: Fall back to kqueue — no io_uring equivalent, but kqueue has some batching via kevent() multi-event submission
- **Feature flag**: `io-uring` enables the Linux fast path
- This bead is primarily a Linux optimization. macOS uses kqueue fallback which is already decent.

## Implementation
```rust
pub struct IoUringMuxPipeline {
    ring: IoUring,
    registered_buffers: Vec<Vec<u8>>,
    registered_fds: Vec<RawFd>,
    pending_ops: HashMap<u64, PendingOp>,
}

// Integration with asupersync via trait
pub trait IoReactor: Send + Sync {
    fn submit_read(&mut self, fd: RawFd, buf: &mut [u8]) -> Token;
    fn submit_write(&mut self, fd: RawFd, buf: &[u8]) -> Token;
    fn reap_completions(&mut self) -> Vec<(Token, io::Result<usize>)>;
}
```

## Tests
- **Criterion benchmarks**: 50 panes, measure syscalls/sec and throughput (before/after)
- Verify zero-copy: trace memory copies with strace (Linux)
- Stress test: 100 panes sustained for 1 hour, no FD leaks, no buffer corruption
- **Fallback test**: verify epoll path works when io_uring unavailable
- **macOS test**: verify kqueue fallback path works correctly
- Isomorphism proof: output is identical regardless of I/O backend

## Acceptance criteria
- io_uring pipeline works on Linux 5.6+
- epoll fallback for older Linux
- kqueue fallback for macOS
- Identical output regardless of backend
- Feature-gated: only compiled when requested
- 2-3x throughput improvement on Linux with io_uring","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-10T17:06:43.093457Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-10T19:34:30.174720Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-283h4.4","depends_on_id":"ft-283h4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-15T21:29:20Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-283h4.4","depends_on_id":"ft-brc7d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-15T21:29:20Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-283h4.4","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-15T21:29:20Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
